evals.prompt: How do I get the newest version of the elite-next plugin? I heard there's a new skill I want to try out.
evals.expected_output: Walks through refreshing the marketplace catalog, asks (or infers) update scope, runs the update command, and verifies the new version.
expectations: Runs or instructs `claude plugin marketplace update elite-next-marketplace` before attempting the update

Either asks which scope (`user` or `project`) to update, or explicitly states which scope it's assuming and offers to switch — doesn't silently pick a scope without flagging the assumption, since the prompt doesn't specify
Runs or instructs `claude plugin update elite-next@elite-next-marketplace` (with `--scope project` if that's the resolved scope)
Suggests verifying the result with `claude plugin list`

evals.prompt: We just merged a PR that bumps the elite-next plugin to 1.6.0 on main. Update it for the whole team on this project, not just for me.
evals.expected_output: Infers project scope from 'whole team' without re-asking, and updates + verifies against version 1.6.0.
expectations: Infers project scope from the 'whole team' phrasing instead of asking a redundant scope question
Runs or instructs `claude plugin update elite-next@elite-next-marketplace --scope project`
Confirms the installed version now matches 1.6.0, e.g. via `claude plugin list`

Explains that project scope alone doesn't push the update to teammates' machines unless the marketplace entry has `autoUpdate: true` — recommends checking for or adding that setting so teammates don't need to manually refresh

evals.prompt: I ran `claude plugin update elite-next@elite-next-marketplace` but it says no update is available. Did I do something wrong?
evals.expected_output: Troubleshoots the stale-catalog and not-yet-merged-version cases before assuming the command itself is broken.
expectations: Suggests re-running `claude plugin marketplace update elite-next-marketplace` to refresh the local catalog
Suggests checking whether the version bump in `plugin.json` has actually been merged to `main` yet
Does not conclude the update command is broken without first ruling out these two causes

evals.prompt: We just merged the PR that bumps the elite-next plugin to 1.6.0. This project's .claude/settings.json already has autoUpdate set for the elite-next-marketplace marketplace — do I need to do anything for the team to pick it up?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes autoUpdate is already configured, so no manual per-teammate refresh is needed once they pull and next start Claude Code — offers the manual commands only as a way to force it immediately, not as a required step.
expectations: Reads .claude/settings.json and recognizes autoUpdate: true is already set for the elite-next-marketplace marketplace
States that teammates will get the update automatically at their next Claude Code startup once they pull the merged change, without needing to run claude plugin marketplace update themselves
Offers the manual `claude plugin marketplace update`/`claude plugin update` commands only as an optional way to force the update immediately, not as a required step

Notes that autoUpdate only takes effect once the version bump has actually reached the branch the marketplace tracks (main) — doesn't assume the merge already reached main just because the user said 'we merged the PR', since the prompt never states which branch
